_comment: Canonical ATT&CK TTP mapping reference for the IR agent. CONSULT this when assigning a finding's mitre_technique; the independent judge cross-checks each finding's TTP against it and flags mismatches. Seeded from VIGIA-REAL-002 + SRL-2015 scoring. Extend as new cases reveal divergences from public answer keys.
principles: When a behavior has multiple defensible technique IDs, prefer the one the public answer key uses (NIST CFReDS / VIGIA / ATT&CK Evals).
Tag every distinct exfiltration CHANNEL separately (email T1048, cloud T1567.002, USB T1052.001, web T1567.001) — do not collapse to one.
Map each finding to the most specific applicable technique/sub-technique.

mappings.behavior: File renamed to a false/different extension to conceal content (e.g. .docx->.mp3, .pptx->.jpg)
mappings.name: Hide Artifacts
mappings.note: VIGIA/NIST keys score this as T1564. T1036.008 (Masquerade File Type) is a defensible alternative but mismatched the key in VIGIA-REAL-002 — prefer T1564 for concealment-by-extension.

mappings.behavior: Data/credentials handed off or exfiltrated via email (e.g. emailing a Drive login, sending files out)
mappings.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
mappings.note: Tag the email channel explicitly even when cloud exfil (T1567) is also present — VIGIA-REAL-002 key listed both; I under-tagged this.

mappings.behavior: Upload to personal cloud storage (Google Drive, Dropbox, personal Gmail)
mappings.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage

mappings.behavior: Logon with a valid account, especially off-hours / policy-violating
mappings.name: Valid Accounts

mappings.behavior: Anti-forensic / cleanup tooling installed or used (CCleaner, Eraser, sdelete); log clearing
mappings.name: Indicator Removal

mappings.behavior: Copying files from a network/secured share
mappings.name: Data from Network Shared Drive

mappings.behavior: Staging collected files locally before exfil (e.g. Desktop\$data)
mappings.name: Local Data Staging

mappings.behavior: Malware running under a fake name/path imitating a system binary (e.g. svchost.exe from a non-System32 dir)
mappings.name: Match Legitimate Name or Location

mappings.behavior: Injected EXECUTE_READWRITE / private memory regions in a process
mappings.name: Process Injection

mappings.behavior: Persistence via registry Run / RunOnce key
mappings.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder

mappings.behavior: C2 over HTTP/HTTPS web protocols (port 443/80 beacon)
mappings.name: Web Protocols

mappings.behavior: Execution via Windows command shell (cmd.exe chains)
mappings.name: Windows Command Shell

mappings.behavior: Spearphishing link / lure (shortened URL directing a user to a malicious or spoofed site)
mappings.name: Spearphishing Link
